diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perioral and acral paraesthesia is an early, characteristic sign of low ionised calcium.
Alkalosis can produce identical paraesthesia — non-specific in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carpopedal spasm / tetany (hands drawing into claw posture)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overt tetany indicates severe symptomatic hypocalcaemia with airway/seizure risk.
Chronic fatigue and weakness are cardinal features of adrenal insufficiency.
Constitutional decline fits a multi-axis endocrine failure.
Postural hypotension reflects mineralocorticoid deficiency / volume depletion in adrenal insufficiency.
Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis is a defining component of type 1 autoimmune polyendocrine syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vitiligo (depigmented skin patches) and alopecia areata

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vitiligo and alopecia areata cluster with organ-specific autoimmunity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss and new cold intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight loss fits adrenal insufficiency; cold intolerance suggests coexisting autoimmune hypothyroidism.
Without hyperventilation there is no respiratory alkalosis to explain the tetany — points to true hypocalcaemia.
Neuromuscular irritability sign of hypocalcaemia — a clinician examination maneuver, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Trousseau sign (carpal spasm on cuff inflation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Specific sign of latent tetany from hypocalcaemia — requires clinician examination.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum corrected calcium and ionised calcium
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The severity of the electrolyte derangement is not quantified and cardiac risk is unassessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low ionised calcium confirms the cause of the tetany — hospital blood test, never available via telehealth.
Inappropriately low PTH in the face of hypocalcaemia confirms hypoparathyroidism as the mechanism.
Hypocalcaemia prolongs the QT interval and predisposes to arrhythmia — a hospital ECG finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Perioral paraesthesia + carpopedal spasm + positive Chvostek/Trousseau is the classic severe-symptomatic-hypocalcaemia signature demanding emergency IV calcium.
Chronic candidiasis + vitiligo/alopecia + constitutional decline + cold intolerance frames the tetany within a multi-axis autoimmune endocrine failure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum corrected + ionised calcium, magnesium, phosphate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the hypocalcaemia driving the tetany; magnesium must be checked as hypomagnesaemia perpetuates hypocalcaemia.
Detects QT prolongation and arrhythmia risk from hypocalcaemia.
Establishes hypoparathyroidism as the cause of the hypocalcaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Morning cortisol / ACTH and thyroid function tests
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens the coexisting adrenal and thyroid axes suggested by fatigue, weight loss and cold intolerance.
Characterises the autoimmune polyendocrine syndrome once the acute crisis is stabilised.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home-measured vital signs without a validated device
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI Doctor should not rely on unavailable or unvalidated home vitals to gate escalation — the clinical tetany pattern already mandates emergency care.
